fact: The first official cricket Test match was played in 1877 between Australia and England at the Melbourne Cricket Ground (MCG). Australia won by 45 runs.
The ICC Cricket World Cup was first held in 1975 in England. West Indies won the inaugural tournament by defeating Australia in the final.
Sachin Tendulkar is the first cricketer to score 100 international centuries.
The Ashes is one of cricket's oldest rivalries, played between England and Australia since 1882.
India won its first ICC Cricket World Cup in 1983 under the captaincy of Kapil Dev.
MS Dhoni is the only captain to win the ICC T20 World Cup, ICC Cricket World Cup, and ICC Champions Trophy.
The Indian Premier League (IPL) began in 2008, with Rajasthan Royals winning the inaugural season.
Brian Lara holds the record for the highest individual score in a Test innings with 400 not out.
Muttiah Muralitharan holds the record for the most wickets in Test cricket with 800 wickets.
Virat Kohli became the fastest batter to score 8,000, 9,000, 10,000, 11,000, 12,000 and 13,000 runs in One Day Internationals.
The ICC Men's T20 World Cup was first held in 2007. India won the inaugural tournament by defeating Pakistan in the final.
England won the 2019 ICC Cricket World Cup after defeating New Zealand in a dramatic final decided by a Super Over.
Australia has won more ICC Men's Cricket World Cup titles than any other country.
The Decision Review System (DRS) was officially introduced in Test cricket in 2008 to help umpires make more accurate decisions.
The ICC World Test Championship was introduced to determine the champion team in Test cricket through a league-based competition.
The Thomas Cup, established in 1948, is the premier international men's team badminton championship. India won its historic first title in 2022 by defeating Indonesia 3-0.
The Uber Cup is the premier international women's team badminton championship and was first held in 1957.
Badminton became an official Olympic sport at the 1992 Barcelona Olympics.
P. V. Sindhu became the first Indian woman to win a silver medal in Olympic badminton at the 2016 Rio Olympics.
P. V. Sindhu won the BWF World Championships title in 2019, becoming the first Indian to become world champion.
Saina Nehwal became the first Indian badminton player to achieve the world number one ranking in women's singles.
Prakash Padukone became the first Indian to win the prestigious All England Open Badminton Championships in 1980.
Kidambi Srikanth became the first Indian man to reach the World Championships final in men's singles.
China has won the Thomas Cup multiple times, making it one of the most successful nations in the tournament's history.
The BWF World Championships are held annually except during Olympic years.
A badminton match is played as the best of three games, with each game played to 21 points under the rally scoring system.
The shuttlecock used in professional badminton is made from 16 goose feathers attached to a cork base.
Viktor Axelsen of Denmark has won Olympic gold medals and multiple BWF World Championship titles.
An Se-young of South Korea has emerged as one of the world's leading women's singles badminton players.
The All England Open Badminton Championships is one of the oldest and most prestigious badminton tournaments in the world.
The FIFA World Cup was first held in 1930. Uruguay hosted and won the tournament, defeating Argentina 4-2 in the final match in Montevideo.
Brazil has won the FIFA World Cup five times, more than any other nation.
The FIFA Women's World Cup was first held in 1991 in China.
Lionel Messi captained Argentina to victory in the 2022 FIFA World Cup.
Cristiano Ronaldo became the first men's footballer to score in five different FIFA World Cups.
The UEFA Champions League is Europe's premier club football competition.
Real Madrid has won more UEFA Champions League titles than any other club.
The Ballon d'Or is awarded annually to the world's best football player.
The Laws of the Game are maintained by the International Football Association Board (IFAB).
The first FIFA World Cup final was played between Uruguay and Argentina.
A standard football match consists of two halves of 45 minutes each, excluding added time.
The offside rule is one of football's fundamental laws and prevents attackers from gaining an unfair positional advantage.
The Video Assistant Referee (VAR) system is used to help referees review important match decisions.
The FIFA World Cup is held every four years and is one of the world's most watched sporting events.
The Copa América is the oldest international continental football tournament.
